sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on the blue block didn't change anything (board_changed: false). Let me try a different approach.
Maybe I need to re-collect the blocks in a different order. Currently the blocks are:
- Red at rows 19-22
- Blue at rows 25-28
- Green at rows 31-34
The desired order (from bottom bar) is: red, green, blue.
So I need:
- Red at rows 19-22
- Green at rows 25-28
- Blue at rows 31-34
To achieve this, I need to:
1. Keep red at rows 19-22 (already correct)
2. Collect green when player is at rows 25-28
3. Collect blue when player is at rows 31-34
But the targets are at fixed positions:
- Red target: rows 19-22 (already collected)
- Blue target: rows 25-28
- Green target: rows 31-34
When I extend the path from rows 25-28, it collects the blue target at rows 25-28. When I extend the path from rows 31-34, it collects the green target at rows 31-34.
So I can't collect green when the player is at rows 25-28, because the path would collect the blue target instead.
Wait, but the blue target has already been collected. So if I extend the path from rows 25-28, there's no blue target to collect. Maybe the path would then collect the green target at rows 31-34?
Let me test this. Move the player to rows 25-28 and extend the path. If the blue target is already collected, maybe the path would collect the green target.
Actually, looking at the current state, the blue target has been collected (it's at rows 25-28, cols 18-21). The green target is still at rows 31-34, cols 42-45.
Wait, but the segmentation shows the green block at rows 31-34, cols 18-21. So the green target has also been collected.
Let me check if the original targets are still on the right side. Looking at the image, there are no targets on the right side. All three targets have been collected.
So the targets are no longer on the right side. This means I can't re-collect them.
Maybe I need to rearrange the collected blocks by clicking on them or moving them with the player.
Let me try moving the player to the blue block's position and see if I can pick it up or move it.
Actually, let me try clicking on the bottom bar blocks to see if that does something.
The bottom bar shows: red, green, blue (from left to right). Maybe I need to click on them in a specific order.
Let me try clicking on the green block on the bottom bar (rows 57-60, cols 33-36). Center is approximately row 58, col 34.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the green block on the bottom bar
# Green block on bottom bar is at rows 57-60, cols 33-36. Center is approximately row 58, col 34.
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 34}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
